items.title: Trellix Confirms Source Code Breach — Unauthorized Repo Access
items.desc: Cybersecurity giant Trellix (ex-McAfee/FireEye) confirms attackers accessed a portion of its source code repository.
items.summary: Trellix disclosed that it 'recently identified' a compromise of its source code repository and engaged forensic experts. The company stated it found no evidence the source code release process was affected or that code has been exploited, but did not disclose attacker identity, access duration, or data exfiltrated. Trellix was formed in 2022 from McAfee Enterprise and FireEye assets; Mandiant was spun off to Google for $5.4B.
items.importance: Source code access at a major security vendor is a worst-case scenario. Attackers with repository access could identify zero-days in Trellix's detection engine, craft evasions, or plant backdoors — undermining trust across thousands of enterprise deployments.
items.noteworthy: Audit Trellix endpoint deployments for unusual behavior. Rotate any API keys or integration tokens. Monitor for Trellix-specific bypasses appearing in threat actor tooling.

items.title: AccountDumpling: 30K Facebook Accounts Hacked via Google AppSheet Phishing
items.desc: Vietnamese-linked op uses Google AppSheet as phishing relay to steal 30,000 Facebook Business accounts, selling them back via illicit storefront.
items.summary: Guardio codenamed the operation 'AccountDumpling,' tracing it to a Vietnamese individual (PHẠM TÀI TÂN). Phishing emails sent from 'noreply@appsheet.com' bypass spam filters, claiming to be Meta Support. Four distinct clusters use Netlify, Vercel, Google Drive PDFs (generated via free Canva), and fake job offers luring victims. Stolen credentials, 2FA codes, and government ID photos are exfiltrated to Telegram channels. Most victims are in the US, Italy, Canada, and the Philippines.
items.importance: Google AppSheet's trusted sender reputation turns it into a perfect phishing relay. This pattern — trusted platforms repurposed as delivery, hosting, and monetization layers — is now the dominant phishing model and near-impossible for users to spot.
items.noteworthy: Block or flag emails from 'noreply@appsheet.com' with Facebook/Meta-themed content. Educate Facebook Business admins: Meta will never threaten immediate deletion. Review Facebook login logs for unrecognized sessions.

items.title: SHADOW-EARTH-053: China-Linked APT Hits 7 Asian Govs + NATO Member
items.desc: China-aligned espionage group exploits N-day Exchange/IIS flaws to deploy ShadowPad across South/SE Asia and Poland.
items.summary: Trend Micro tracks the cluster as SHADOW-EARTH-053, active since December 2024, overlapping with CL-STA-0049 and Earth Alux. Targets include Pakistan, Thailand, Malaysia, India, Myanmar, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, and Poland (NATO). The group exploits ProxyLogon chain and React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182) on unpatched Exchange/IIS servers, drops Godzilla webshells, and deploys ShadowPad via AnyDesk DLL sideloading. Linux Noodle RAT variants observed. Uses IOX, GOST, Wstunnel tunneling and Mimikatz for credential theft.
items.importance: Poland's inclusion signals Chinese APTs are now actively targeting NATO member states, not just regional neighbors. N-day exploitation means unpatched Exchange/IIS servers remain the primary entry vector — and there are thousands.
items.noteworthy: Patch Exchange and IIS immediately. Deploy IPS/WAF rules for ProxyLogon, ProxyShell, and React2Shell exploit patterns. Hunt for Godzilla webshell IOCs. Segment IIS servers from internal networks.

items.title: Vishing-to-SaaS: Cordial Spider & Snarky Spider in Rapid Extortion Blitz
items.desc: Two cybercrime groups use vishing + SSO AiTM to compromise entire SaaS ecosystems in hours, leaving minimal forensic trace.
items.summary: CrowdStrike identified Cordial Spider (aka BlackFile, UNC6671) and Snarky Spider (aka UNC6661, tied to 'The Com' e-crime ecosystem), active since October 2025. Attackers call targets impersonating IT support, direct them to SSO-themed AiTM phishing pages, capture credentials + MFA, and register new devices to maintain access while deleting MFA notifications via inbox rules. Pivot to scraping employee directories for high-privilege accounts, then exfiltrate from Google Workspace, HubSpot, SharePoint, and Salesforce.
items.importance: This is extortion at SaaS speed. By staying entirely within trusted SaaS environments and using residential proxies, these groups are nearly invisible to traditional network detection. The IdP becomes a single point of compromise for every connected SaaS app.
items.noteworthy: Enable device registration alerts that can't be auto-deleted by inbox rules. Monitor for new device registrations followed by mass file downloads. Implement FIDO2/WebAuthn to defeat AiTM phishing. Train help desk to verify caller identity out-of-band.

items.title: BufferZoneCorp: Poisoned Ruby Gems & Go Modules Exploit CI Pipelines
items.desc: Sleepers in RubyGems and Go proxy registries harvest AWS keys, SSH creds, and GitHub Actions tokens from CI runners.
items.summary: Socket uncovered a campaign by GitHub account 'BufferZoneCorp' publishing malicious Ruby gems (masquerading as activesupport-logger, devise-jwt) and Go modules (go-retryablehttp, grpc-client). Ruby gems harvest environment variables, SSH keys, AWS secrets, .npmrc, and GitHub CLI config at install time, exfiltrating to Webhook.site. Go modules tamper with GitHub Actions workflows via a fake go binary wrapper, append attacker SSH keys to authorized_keys, and steal developer data.
items.importance: CI pipelines hold the keys to the kingdom — cloud creds, signing keys, deployment tokens. A single poisoned dependency in a CI runner can compromise every artifact that pipeline produces, making this a force-multiplier supply chain attack.
items.noteworthy: Audit RubyGems and Go module dependencies for suspicious packages. Check ~/.ssh/authorized_keys for unknown entries. Rotate all CI/CD secrets if any suspicious package was installed. Review GitHub Actions run logs for unexpected go binary paths.

items.title: Copy Fail: CVE-2026-31431 — 2017 Kernel Bug Gives Root on All Linux Distros
items.desc: 732-byte Python script exploits 9-year-old kernel crypto flaw for instant root on Amazon Linux, RHEL, SUSE, Ubuntu.
items.summary: Xint.io and Theori disclosed CVE-2026-31431 (CVSS 7.8), a local privilege escalation in the Linux kernel's algif_aead crypto module, introduced in August 2017. An unprivileged user can write 4 controlled bytes into the page cache of any readable file—including setuid binaries. The exploit is portable, tiny, reliable (no race condition), and works cross-container since the page cache is shared. Major distros have released patched kernels.
items.importance: This is Dirty Pipe 2.0 — same class of page-cache corruption primitive, different subsystem. A reliable, single-script LPE on every Linux server shipped since 2017 is a pentester's dream and a defender's nightmare. Cross-container impact means container escapes are trivial.
items.noteworthy: Patch kernel immediately per your distro's advisory (Amazon Linux, Arch, Debian, RHEL, SUSE, Ubuntu all have fixes). Audit for unexpected setuid binaries. In containerized environments, assume any container compromise = host compromise until patched.

items.title: PromptMink: DPRK Used Claude Opus to Inject Malware into npm Package
items.desc: North Korea's Famous Chollima group had Claude Opus commit a malicious npm dependency into an autonomous crypto trading agent.
items.summary: ReversingLabs codenamed the campaign 'PromptMink,' linking it to DPRK's Famous Chollima (aka Shifty Corsair, behind Contagious Interview). Claude Opus co-authored a commit on Feb 28 that added '@validate-sdk/v2' as a dependency to 'openpaw-graveyard' — an autonomous Solana trading agent. The package was a cryptocurrency stealer, part of a two-layer supply chain with benign first-layer packages (e.g., @solana-launchpad/sdk) importing malicious second-layer ones. The campaign has evolved to target PyPI and use SSH persistence + Rust-compiled payloads for full project exfiltration.
items.importance: AI-assisted supply chain attacks are no longer theoretical. A nation-state actor literally had an LLM write and commit malware into a project. This blurs the line between 'vibe-coding accident' and intentional backdoor, making attribution and detection vastly harder.
items.noteworthy: Audit all AI-generated commits and pull requests — especially package additions. Scan for the listed malicious npm packages. Verify npm audit/CI passes. Treat AI-authored code with the same scrutiny as untrusted external contributions.

items.title: Two Ransomware Negotiators Get 4 Years for BlackCat Attacks
items.desc: US sentences two cybersecurity professionals who flipped from defenders to BlackCat ransomware affiliates.
items.summary: Two individuals who previously worked as ransomware negotiators were sentenced to 4 years in US federal prison for their roles in BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware attacks. The case highlights the insider threat risk within the incident response ecosystem, where professionals with intimate knowledge of negotiation tactics and victim psychology repurposed their skills for criminal gain.
items.importance: When the people you hire to negotiate with ransomware gangs are themselves working for those gangs, the entire IR trust model collapses. This case should prompt every org to vet their IR vendors more aggressively.
items.noteworthy: Vet incident response firms and individual consultants. Ask vendors about employee background checks and insider threat programs. Review any past BlackCat negotiations for signs of collusion.

items.title: Deep#Door RAT: New Stealthy Backdoor Targets Windows with Kernel Tricks
items.desc: Newly discovered Deep#Door RAT combines kernel-level stealth, persistence mechanisms, and multi-stage payload delivery.
items.summary: Security researchers disclosed Deep#Door, a new Remote Access Trojan targeting Windows systems with advanced stealth and persistence. The malware uses kernel-level techniques to hide its presence, deploys through multi-stage delivery chains, and maintains persistence via multiple redundant mechanisms, making removal difficult even for experienced defenders.
items.importance: Kernel-level stealth in commodity RATs signals a democratization of advanced techniques previously reserved for nation-state APTs. As detection tools improve, malware authors are pushing deeper into the stack to evade them.
items.noteworthy: Update EDR signatures for Deep#Door IOCs. Monitor for unusual kernel driver loads. Hunt for the multi-stage delivery chain indicators. If found, assume full system compromise — reimage affected machines.

items.title: BTC at $78.3K — 100-EMA Holds, Options Market Pricing 25% $84K Odds
items.desc: Bitcoin rebounds from 100-day EMA support at $78.3K; futures OI rises 6.6% to 257K BTC as traders position for upside.
items.summary: Bitcoin gained 2.5% to trade at $78,300, rebounding off the 100-day exponential moving average. Spot market buy volumes strengthened and cumulative volume delta turned positive. BTC futures open interest rose 6.64% to 257,000 BTC, indicating fresh positioning. Options markets price only a 25% probability of BTC hitting $84K in May. Ethereum trades at $2,304 (+1%), Solana at $83.97 (flat).
items.importance: The 100-EMA hold is technically significant — it's been reliable support. But options pricing 25% $84K odds shows the market isn't convinced the rally has legs. The rising OI without price acceleration could mean shorts are building.
items.noteworthy: Watch $78K (100-EMA) as key support — a breakdown targets $74K. Break above $80.5K resistance needed for bullish confirmation. OI surge without price follow-through = caution. Monitor Coinbase Premium for US demand signals.

items.title: CLARITY Act Stablecoin Yield Rules Finalized — 55% Odds of 2026 Passage
items.desc: US Senate Banking Committee finalizes stablecoin yield provisions; Polymarket odds jump 9% as Coinbase calls for markup.
items.summary: Senators Tillis and Alsobrooks published the final stablecoin yield provisions for the CLARITY Act. The text (SEC 404) bans interest/yield on stablecoins akin to bank deposits but allows rewards tied to 'bona fide activities.' Banks won additional restrictions on rewards. Coinbase CLO Faryar Shirzad called it 'go time' and CEO Brian Armstrong urged markup. Polymarket odds for 2026 passage rose 9% to 55%. Galaxy Digital's Alex Thorn expects Senate Banking markup as soon as May 11.
items.importance: CLARITY is the most consequential US crypto bill since the FIT21 framework. The stablecoin yield compromise unblocks the biggest legislative logjam. If passed, it creates a federal regulatory floor for crypto — the end of regulation-by-enforcement.
items.noteworthy: Track Senate Banking Committee schedule for week of May 11. Position crypto holdings for regulatory tailwinds if markup proceeds. Stablecoin issuers: begin preparing compliance frameworks for yield restrictions.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation Sells Another 10K ETH to BitMine in 3rd OTC Deal
items.desc: Ethereum Foundation executes third OTC sale of 10,000 ETH to BitMine, continuing pattern of large strategic liquidations.
items.summary: The Ethereum Foundation sold another 10,000 ETH (~$23M) to institutional miner BitMine in the third such OTC transaction. The pattern of regular large sales by the Foundation, while likely part of a treasury management strategy, creates consistent sell pressure on ETH price. ETH trades at $2,304, underperforming BTC during the current rally.
items.importance: The Foundation's continued selling at $2.3K ETH signals they see this as an acceptable liquidation price. If the pattern continues, it creates a de facto ceiling on ETH rallies — institutional buyers know more supply is coming.
items.noteworthy: Monitor Ethereum Foundation wallet addresses for the next scheduled sale. Consider ETH/BTC ratio as a signal — persistent underperformance likely continues while Foundation selling persists. BTC remains the stronger relative bet.

items.title: GitHub RCE: CVE-2026-3854 — One Git Push, Full Server Compromise
items.desc: Wiz uncovers critical RCE in GitHub's git infrastructure — exploitable by any authenticated user via a single git push.
items.summary: Wiz Research disclosed CVE-2026-3854, a critical remote code execution vulnerability in GitHub's internal git pipeline. Any authenticated user could execute arbitrary commands on GitHub's backend servers with a single 'git push' using a standard git client. On GitHub.com, this allowed RCE on shared storage nodes where millions of public and private repositories were accessible. On GitHub Enterprise Server, the same flaw grants full server compromise including access to all repos and internal secrets. Notably, this was one of the first critical vulnerabilities found in closed-source binaries using AI-augmented reverse engineering (IDA MCP). GitHub mitigated GitHub.com within 6 hours, but 88% of GHES instances remain unpatched.
items.importance: This is a supply chain nuclear bomb. GitHub hosts hundreds of millions of repos — a single RCE in the git pipeline means an attacker could poison builds, inject backdoors, or exfiltrate source code across the entire platform. The AI-assisted discovery method signals that closed-source binary security is now trivially auditable.
items.noteworthy: Patch GHES immediately to 3.14.24+, 3.15.19+, 3.16.15+, 3.17.12+, 3.18.6+, or 3.19.3+. Audit git push logs for anomalous payloads. Rotate all repository secrets. Run 'ghe-repl-status' and verify cluster integrity.

items.title: CPanel Critical Auth Bypass — Update Immediately
items.desc: Critical authentication vulnerability in cPanel allows unauthorized access to server management interfaces.
items.summary: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability has been identified in cPanel, one of the most widely used web hosting control panels. The flaw could allow attackers to bypass authentication and gain unauthorized access to server management interfaces, potentially leading to full server compromise across shared hosting environments.
items.importance: cPanel powers millions of hosting environments worldwide. An auth bypass here means attackers can take over web servers, intercept email, and pivot into databases — all without credentials. Shared hosting providers are especially exposed.
items.noteworthy: Update cPanel immediately. Audit access logs for unauthorized login attempts. Review cPanel service tokens and reset any that look suspicious.

items.title: CISA KEV Update: ConnectWise & Windows Flaws Actively Exploited
items.desc: CISA adds actively exploited ConnectWise and Windows vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.
items.summary: CISA has added newly confirmed actively exploited vulnerabilities in ConnectWise and Microsoft Windows to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog. Federal agencies face binding remediation deadlines, but the active exploitation status means private-sector organizations are equally at risk.
items.importance: CISA only adds to KEV when exploitation is confirmed in the wild. ConnectWise remote access tools are ubiquitous in MSP environments — compromise here cascades to every managed client. Windows flaws hitting KEV mean threat actors are actively weaponizing them.
items.noteworthy: Patch ConnectWise and Windows immediately per CISA directives. If you're an MSP, assume lateral movement to all client environments. Check ConnectWise audit logs for unauthorized sessions.

items.title: LiteLLM SQL Injection Exploited Within 36 Hours
items.desc: CVE-2026-42208 in LiteLLM — SQL injection weaponized just 36 hours after disclosure.
items.summary: A SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-42208) in LiteLLM, a popular LLM gateway/proxy used to manage API calls across multiple AI providers, was exploited in the wild within 36 hours of disclosure. The speed from disclosure to mass exploitation demonstrates how quickly AI-adjacent infrastructure is targeted.
items.importance: LiteLLM sits in front of LLM API keys for many organizations — a SQL injection here means attackers can exfiltrate API keys, manipulate model routing, or inject responses. The 36-hour weaponization window shows AI infra is a priority target.
items.noteworthy: Update LiteLLM immediately. Rotate all LLM API keys that passed through the gateway. Audit query logs for SQL injection patterns.

items.title: VECT 2.0 Ransomware: Destructive Wiper Disguised as Ransomware
items.desc: New VECT 2.0 ransomware irreversibly destroys files over 131KB — no decryption possible.
items.summary: VECT 2.0 ransomware has been identified targeting Windows, Linux, and ESXi environments. Unlike traditional ransomware, files over 131KB are irreversibly destroyed rather than encrypted — making recovery impossible even if a ransom is paid. This positions VECT 2.0 as a wiper with ransomware camouflage, likely intended for disruption rather than profit.
items.importance: When ransomware destroys instead of encrypts, backups are your only recovery path — and threat actors know this. VECT 2.0 suggests a shift toward purely destructive campaigns, possibly nation-state aligned, where the ransom note is just a distraction.
items.noteworthy: Verify offline/immutable backups are intact. EDR rules targeting VECT 2.0 IOCs. Segment ESXi management networks. If hit, do not pay — decryption is impossible.

items.title: Intel Arc Pro B70: 32GB VRAM AI Workstation GPU Launches
items.desc: Intel doubles down on AI GPU market with Arc Pro B70 featuring 32GB VRAM and Xe2 architecture.
items.summary: Intel launched the Arc Pro B70, a new professional GPU built on the Xe2 architecture with 32GB of VRAM — double the cores and memory of the B50. Unlike AMD's approach of retrofitting gaming GPUs with extra VRAM (Radeon AI PRO R9700), Intel built an entirely new die. The B70 features ECC memory, dual media engines (8K 10-bit encode/decode), certified pro drivers for Adobe/Autodesk/Dassault, and targets the AI workstation market alongside NVIDIA's 72GB RTX PRO 5000 Blackwell.
items.importance: The AI workstation GPU market is now a three-horse race. Intel's aggressive pricing and full-die approach could disrupt NVIDIA's margins in the mid-range AI workstation tier. 32GB VRAM at this price point makes local LLM inference accessible to smaller teams.
items.noteworthy: Evaluate the B70 for local AI inference workloads if you're budget-constrained vs NVIDIA's 72GB Blackwell. Check driver maturity for your specific apps — Intel's pro driver certification program is still catching up to NVIDIA's.

items.title: Gallium Oxide Semiconductors Withstand Near-Absolute-Zero Cold
items.desc: KAUST research shows gallium oxide electronics operate reliably near absolute zero — enabling space and quantum computing applications.
items.summary: Researchers at KAUST demonstrated that gallium oxide (Ga₂O₃) semiconductor devices maintain robust operation at cryogenic temperatures close to absolute zero. This is a breakthrough for space missions and quantum computing control electronics, where traditional silicon devices fail at extreme cold. Ga₂O₃'s ultra-wide bandgap provides inherent radiation hardness and thermal stability.
items.importance: Quantum computing requires control electronics that function at cryogenic temperatures — current solutions need complex room-temperature wiring to quantum processors. Ga₂O₃ could enable co-located control circuits, drastically reducing latency and complexity. Space and defense applications are immediate beneficiaries.
items.noteworthy: Monitor Ga₂O₃ foundry partnerships. If your org runs HPC in extreme environments or quantum R&D, this semiconductor could reduce cooling and wiring overhead within 3-5 years.

items.title: ASML: The Chokepoint for Cutting-Edge Chips
items.desc: By betting on EUV lithography before it worked, ASML became the single bottleneck for advanced chipmaking worldwide.
items.summary: A deep analysis of ASML's position as the sole manufacturer of extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography machines — the only equipment capable of producing sub-5nm chips. ASML's decades-long bet on EUV, when the rest of the industry doubted it, made them the gatekeeper of advanced semiconductor manufacturing. No foundry (TSMC, Samsung, Intel) can produce cutting-edge chips without ASML's machines.
items.importance: Geopolitical control over ASML's export decisions directly determines which countries can manufacture advanced chips. US restrictions on ASML exports to China are effectively a semiconductor embargo. Any disruption to ASML (supply chain, sabotage, regulation) would halt global advanced chip production.
items.noteworthy: Factor ASML export restrictions into long-term hardware procurement planning. If you rely on TSMC-fabbed chips, any ASML disruption cascades to your supply within 6-18 months.

items.title: BTC at $76.6K — Fed Chair Warsh Signals Mixed for Risk Assets
items.desc: Bitcoin holds $76.6K as new Fed chair Kevin Warsh takes helm; historical pattern suggests 'months of losses.'
items.summary: Bitcoin trades at $76,596 with a modest +0.6% 24h change, but under pressure as Kevin Warsh becomes the new Federal Reserve chair. Historical data shows BTC fell after each new Fed chair began their term. The Coinbase Premium Index turned negative for the first time in three weeks, signaling weakening US spot demand. Weekly liquidations topped $829M, adding to bearish momentum even as long-term holders accumulate.
items.importance: A new Fed chair historically brings policy uncertainty that compresses risk assets. Warsh's mixed signals on rate policy mean BTC could face a multi-month drawdown. The negative Coinbase Premium confirms institutional US buyers are stepping back.
items.noteworthy: Monitor the Fed chair transition window — if historical pattern holds, BTC may trend lower for 2-4 months before recovering. Negative Coinbase Premium = weak US demand. Watch for accumulation by long-term holders as a contrarian bottom signal.

items.title: Canada Proposes Crypto ATM Ban Over Money Laundering
items.desc: Ottawa moves to ban Bitcoin ATMs, citing their role as a key tool for scammers and money laundering.
items.summary: The Canadian government proposed banning crypto ATMs entirely, arguing they have become a primary instrument for scammers and money launderers. The ban is part of broader regulatory tightening targeting high-risk segments of the crypto sector, following increasing reports of fraud facilitated through physical crypto ATMs.
items.importance: If Canada bans crypto ATMs, other jurisdictions may follow. This directly impacts on-ramp accessibility for retail users and could set a precedent for physical crypto infrastructure regulation worldwide.
items.noteworthy: If you operate or use crypto ATMs in Canada, prepare for shutdown. Monitor US and EU regulators for similar proposals. The trend is clear: physical crypto access points are being regulated like traditional financial infrastructure.

items.title: Hugging Face LeRobot Open to Unauthenticated RCE
items.desc: Critical unpatched CVE-2026-25874 in Hugging Face's LeRobot framework allows unauthenticated remote code execution.
items.summary: A critical unpatched vulnerability (CVE-2026-25874) in Hugging Face's LeRobot — a popular open-source robotics/AI framework — allows unauthenticated remote code execution. With no patch available at time of disclosure, any system running LeRobot exposed to the network is vulnerable to full takeover.
items.importance: LeRobot is used in AI/robotics research and prototyping environments — often on under-protected lab networks. An unauthenticated RCE with no patch means researchers' machines and potentially connected robotics hardware are at immediate risk of compromise.
items.noteworthy: Isolate LeRobot instances from the internet immediately. Network-segment any robotics lab environments. Monitor for patches from Hugging Face. Do not expose LeRobot ports publicly.

items.title: Microsoft: Windows Shell CVE-2026-32202 Actively Exploited
items.desc: Microsoft confirms Windows Shell vulnerability is being actively exploited in the wild.
items.summary: Microsoft confirmed that CVE-2026-32202, a vulnerability in Windows Shell, is being actively exploited. The flaw allows attackers to execute code through malicious file handling in the Windows Shell. Active exploitation confirmation means threat actors are already using this in campaigns, making patching an immediate priority.
items.importance: Windows Shell vulnerabilities are high-impact because they affect virtually every Windows machine and often require minimal user interaction. Active exploitation + broad attack surface = urgent patching required.
items.noteworthy: Deploy Microsoft's patch immediately via WSUS/Intune. Monitor for suspicious Shell process spawns. BlockKnown exploit vectors via ASR rules if patching is delayed.
